title: Locale catalog overlays (trackable manual patches)
description: Hand-rolled overlay file applied at Tier 2 build time on top of Tier 1 src-data. Used to inject locale tags that CLDR availableLocales does not ship but the platform needs (e.g., fr-TF — French Southern Territories has French as administrative primary locale; CLDR's availableLocales has no fr-TF entry) AND to override / remove specific entries when upstream is wrong. Each entry carries a `reason` + `addedAt` so the policy decision is audit-trail visible. The Tier 2 builder loads overlays after Tier 1 src-data and merges them in before running cross-catalog M:M backfill.

additions.description: Locale tags to ADD that CLDR availableLocales does not ship but the platform needs (e.g., fr-TF). Each entry's `data` must match the Tier 1 SrcDataLocale shape (the Tier 2 builder handles cross-catalog M:M backfill + denormalization).

overrides.description: Field-level patches to existing Tier 1 entries. Use sparingly — prefer fixing upstream when possible.

removals.description: Tier 1 entries to drop from the Tier 2 output. Use rarely — usually a sign the upstream source has a wrong entry we don't want to ship.

id.description: Primary key — the IETF BCP 47 tag (e.g., 'fr-TF', 'en-XK'). Matches `data.ietfBcp47Tag` exactly.

data.description: Full Tier 1 SrcDataLocale shape. The Tier 2 builder ingests this exactly as if it had come from Tier 1 src-data, then runs the standard cross-catalog M:M backfill + denormalization pipeline.

regionSubtag.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 (UPPERCASE) OR UN M49 numeric (3 digits), OR null when the tag is bare-language. Matches Tier 1 SrcDataLocale.regionSubtag shape.

fields.description: Partial Tier 1 SrcDataLocale — only the fields being patched. Missing fields stay as-is from Tier 1.
